Branding, websites, and growth strategy built specifically for Direct Primary Care and concierge practices.

IndustryHealthcare growth & branding

Webitsjumpstart.com
01 · The Foundation

Who we are, and who we are for

JumpStart DPC Solutions is a healthcare branding and growth partner built for one world: independent primary care. We help physicians launch, brand, and grow Direct Primary Care and concierge practices that patients understand and want to join. Most agencies treat a DPC launch like any other small business. We do not. We know what it takes to explain a membership model to a skeptical patient, to fill a panel before opening day, and to stand out locally without sounding like a hospital ad.

Mission

Champion the growth of DPC nationwide.

We help independent physicians launch, grow, and sustain DPC and concierge practices through clear branding, strategic marketing, custom websites, and practical growth support.

Vision

Make independent care easy to find.

A version of primary care where any physician who wants to practice independently can build a real business around it, and any patient looking for that kind of care can find it. We want DPC to stop being the best kept secret in medicine.

Positioning

The specialist, not the generalist.

For physicians launching or growing a DPC or concierge practice, JumpStart turns a clinical vision into a brand patients trust and a practice they want to join. We work only in independent primary care, so the strategy is built for the model from the start.

Value proposition

JumpStart gives independent physicians a complete brand and growth engine, positioning, a website that converts, SEO, and ongoing marketing, built specifically for DPC and concierge practices, so they can fill their panel and grow without becoming marketers themselves.

The one-liner

We help physicians build practices that patients understand, trust, and want to join.

That sentence is the whole brand. Not "we make logos and websites." Everything JumpStart produces, a homepage, a brochure, a cold email, a conference banner, should move a physician closer to a patient who gets the model and signs up. If a piece of work does not do that, it is decoration.

02 · Audience

Who we serve

Primary audience

  • Physicians starting a DPC practice
  • Concierge medicine practices
  • Independent primary care practices
  • Nurse practitioner led practices in concierge or cash-pay models
  • Practices moving from insurance-based care to DPC
  • Existing DPC practices that need better branding, a better website, or more consistent marketing

Secondary audience

  • DPC organizations and networks
  • Healthcare conferences and event partners
  • Physician communities
  • DPC vendors and technology partners
  • Practices launching new service lines
Ideal client profile

A physician (MD, DO, or NP led) who is leaving or has left insurance-based medicine to start a DPC or concierge practice, or an established independent practice that has the clinical side handled but is held back by weak branding, a generic website, or inconsistent marketing. They care about quality and trust, dislike anything that feels corporate or salesy, and want a partner who already understands the model so they do not have to explain it.

What we hear from clients

"I know the care I want to provide. I do not know how to explain it clearly."

"I need a professional website that makes people trust me, not a cookie cutter medical template."

"Patients do not understand DPC yet, and I need members before I open."

"I do not have time to run marketing myself, and I want someone who actually knows the model."

Other recurring pain points we solve: marketing that does not sound salesy, naming and positioning help, local visibility and search, and converting interest into membership inquiries.

03 · Personality

How the brand carries itself

JumpStart should feel like a trusted advisor who understands both the physician side and the patient side. Experienced, practical, and human. The kind of partner who tells you the truth and then does the work.

StrategicClearGrounded Healthcare-specificPracticalConfident ApproachableModernTrustworthy Founder-ledGrowth-orientedDPC-native

We are

  • Honest and direct, even when it is not what a client hoped to hear
  • Specific to independent healthcare, not borrowed from other industries
  • Confident without arrogance, strategic without jargon
  • Warm, but never fluffy

We are not

  • Corporate, sterile, or buzzword heavy
  • Gimmicky or startup-flashy
  • A generic agency that took a healthcare client
  • Vague. We do not hide behind words like innovative, transform, or elevate
04 · Brand Voice

How we sound

Clear, direct, helpful, and smart. We write in plain language a busy physician or a curious patient can scan. We respect the reader. If a sentence sounds like a generic agency or like an AI wrote it, it is wrong.

Don't say

"We leverage cutting-edge digital solutions to optimize patient acquisition."

Say

"We help patients understand who you are, what you offer, and why your practice is worth joining."

Don't say

"We build beautiful websites."

Say

"We build websites that explain your model, build trust, and turn interest into membership inquiries."

Don't say

"Marketing services for healthcare providers."

Say

"Branding, websites, and growth strategy built specifically for DPC and concierge practices."

Don't say

"Our innovative, end-to-end platform empowers providers to transform the patient journey."

Say

"From your first patient to a full panel, we handle the branding, the website, and the marketing so you can focus on care."

05 · Messaging Pillars

The five things we say everywhere

01

Specialized in DPC

We understand the DPC model, the patient education problem, and the business realities of independent practice growth. You do not have to teach us the space.

02

Strategy before design

Good branding is not just a logo. It starts with positioning, audience, messaging, and what makes your practice different. We get that right first.

03

Websites that convert

We build websites that explain the practice clearly, establish trust, answer the questions patients actually ask, and drive membership inquiries.

04

Practical growth support

Not theory. Real execution: SEO, blogs, social posts, local outreach, referral materials, and launch campaigns that keep running after launch day.

05

Independent practice advocacy

JumpStart exists to support physicians who want to practice medicine differently and build sustainable independent practices. That belief sits under everything we make.

06 · What We Do

The full arc of a practice

We start with strategy, then build the brand, the website, and the marketing on top of it. Most clients work with us through launch and stay for ongoing growth.

Brand positioning & strategy

Positioning workshop, ideal patient profile, competitive differentiation, messaging framework, brand voice, tagline direction, value proposition, and employer-facing positioning where it fits.

Branding

Name refinement, logo direction, color palette, typography, full visual identity, brand guidelines, and Canva-ready assets with a practice-specific look and feel.

Website build

Custom Duda websites with patient-centered structure, clear calls to action, a plain explanation of the DPC model, local optimization, launch pages, and membership-focused conversion.

SEO & AEO

SEO setup, meta titles and descriptions, clean URL slugs, blog strategy and refreshes, local SEO, and Answer Engine Optimization for AI and search platforms.

Ongoing marketing

Social strategy, blog content, email campaigns, local outreach and referral materials, employer marketing, membership growth campaigns, and ongoing strategy calls.

Consulting

DPC launch strategy, practice messaging, patient acquisition, pricing presentation, employer outreach, launch timelines, and marketing prioritization.

07 · The Logo

A heart, drawn with a heartbeat through it

The mark is a single continuous line: a heart with a pulse reading running across it. It says healthcare and vitality without resorting to clipart, and the JumpStart idea lands in the most literal way possible, a heart getting going. The wordmark sits in a clean geometric sans. This is the identity. Use it as delivered. Do not rebuild it in another typeface or redraw the mark.

Primary lockup · horizontal

The default. Use this version wherever there is room: website headers, email, letterhead, proposals, social banners. Icon left, name right. Always on cream, white, or another light, calm background.

Stacked lockup

For tight or square spaces: social profile headers, ads, business cards, conference signage. Use when the horizontal version would have to shrink too far to fit.

Reverse · white

For green or dark photographic backgrounds. The all-white version keeps the mark legible without competing for color. Never place the color logo on green.

The icon, on its own

The heart-pulse mark works alone as a favicon, app icon, social avatar, or a small stamp on a document corner. Keep it in orange on light surfaces, white on green, and give it room to breathe.

Orange on cream · favicon, light UI, document corners

White on green · avatars, app tiles, dark UI

Orange on tan · quiet, warm accent uses only

Logo do

  • Give the mark clear space on all sides equal to the height of the icon
  • Default to color on light, white on green or dark
  • Use the single-color black version only for fax, stamps, or one-color print
  • Keep the icon and wordmark in their delivered proportions

Logo don't

  • Do not stretch, squash, rotate, or recolor the mark
  • Do not rebuild the wordmark in Fraunces or any other font
  • Do not add drop shadows, outlines, or gradients
  • Do not place the color logo on green, or any version on a busy photo without a solid backing

Color, white, black, horizontal, stacked, and standalone icon files are delivered alongside this kit as clean transparent PNGs.

08 · Color

Green for trust. Orange for energy. Cream to breathe.

Deep green carries trust, calm, and healthcare credibility. Orange is the accent, never the whole page. Cream keeps everything light and human. Lead with green and cream; reach for orange when something needs to move.

Deep Sage Green
#4D5C4C
Primary anchor. Headlines, dark bands, footers, primary logo color, navigation.
Warm Orange
#DB7E41
Accent only. Buttons, links, key highlights, the single energy point in a layout.
Brand Cream
#E8E4D7
Primary background. The page should feel like warm paper, not white.
Soft Muted Green
#97A896
Secondary green. Supporting shapes, secondary icons, subtle fills and borders.
Warm Tan / Gold
#EFBE7D
Secondary accent. Quiet highlights, badges, tags, supporting illustration.
Light Gray
#D9D9D9
Dividers, hairlines, table rules, disabled states. Use sparingly.

Suggested balance on a page

Cream 50% Green 30% Sage 12% Orange 8%

Roughly half the surface is cream, a third is green, and orange stays under ten percent. If a layout feels loud, the orange is doing too much.

09 · Typography

A warm serif, paired with a clean sans

The pairing gives JumpStart its premium-but-human feel: a characterful serif for headlines, a quiet humanist sans for everything that needs to be read fast. Both are free, web-ready, and available in Canva and Google Fonts.

Display · Headlines
Fraunces
Aa Bb Cc · 1234567890

Warm, optical, slightly editorial. Used for hero lines, section titles, and pull quotes. Weights 400 to 600.

Use for: heroes, headings, quotes, the wordmark

Close alternative if needed: Newsreader.

Body · Interface
Hanken Grotesk
Aa Bb Cc · 1234567890

Clean, friendly, and highly readable at small sizes. Used for body copy, labels, buttons, and captions. Weights 400 to 700.

Use for: paragraphs, UI, labels, captions

Close alternative if needed: Figtree or Mulish.

How this sits with the logo

Your logo wordmark is set in a clean geometric sans (close to Montserrat) and stays exactly as delivered. Fraunces is the brand's editorial display face for headlines, decks, and marketing, where it adds a warmth a geometric sans cannot. The two coexist on purpose: the logo is the stamp, Fraunces is the voice. If you would rather run a single-personality system, the alternative is to set headlines in a geometric sans that echoes the logo (Montserrat or Poppins) and keep Hanken Grotesk for body.

Type scale

H1 / HeroPractices patients join
H2 / SectionBuilt for the DPC model
H3 / CardWebsites that convert
EyebrowBranding & Growth
BodyWe help patients understand who you are and why your practice is worth joining.
CaptionDirect Primary Care · concierge medicine

For email and any environment without the brand fonts, fall back to a clean serif (Georgia) for display and a neutral sans for body. Keep the hierarchy and spacing the same.

10 · Visual Style

Healthcare-adjacent, never sterile

Overall feel

Clean, modern, and quietly premium. Generous white space, simple shapes, and one clear point of focus per layout. Professional but warm. Not techy, not childish, not corporate blue-and-gray.

Photography

Real physicians, real patients, real places over stock. Warm natural light and candid moments. Skip the staged handshake and the stethoscope-on-a-keyboard cliche. Grade images warm so they sit with the palette.

Iconography

Simple line icons, single consistent stroke weight, rounded joints, to echo the logo's own line. Draw from the brand motifs: heartbeat, pulse, heart, connection. Avoid literal medical clipart and glossy 3D icons.

Motifs we use

HeartbeatPulseVitality CareForward motionGrowth ConnectionPatient trustIndependent care

Use the heartbeat line from the logo as a recurring graphic device: a single thin line that runs across a layout with one clean beat in it. Pull it out of the mark to underline a header, divide a section, or trail off the edge of a page. It ties the website, decks, and print back to the logo. Keep rocket and literal launch imagery off the table. The energy in this brand comes from the pulse, not a countdown.

Website design direction

  • Cream backgrounds, green structure, orange CTAs
  • A plain-language explanation of DPC near the top of the homepage
  • One clear action per section, usually toward a membership inquiry
  • Real photos of the practice, the people, and the place
  • Pricing and the model shown openly, no insurance hedging
  • Fast, mobile-first, built clean in Duda

Social media direction

  • Consistent templates: cream or green base, serif headline, sans body
  • One idea per post, written like a person, not a brochure
  • Educational and myth-busting content about how DPC works
  • Orange reserved for the single key word or the CTA
  • Founder voice and behind-the-practice moments over stock graphics
  • Square and vertical sizes set up once as reusable Canva templates
11 · Canva Brand Kit Setup

What to load into Canva today

Brand kit values

  • Colors: #4D5C4C, #DB7E41, #E8E4D7, #97A896, #EFBE7D, #D9D9D9
  • Headline font: Fraunces (500–600)
  • Body font: Hanken Grotesk (400–700)
  • Logo: upload the horizontal and stacked lockups, the white reverse versions, and the heart-pulse icon
  • Tagline: Simple. Tailored. Effective.

Templates to build once

  • Instagram square and story post templates
  • LinkedIn post and banner
  • One-pager / service flyer
  • Proposal cover and section dividers
  • Conference banner, table runner, and rack card
  • Email header and signature block
12 · Do's and Don'ts

The quick reference

Do

  • Lead with patient understanding and trust, not features
  • Write in plain language a busy reader can scan
  • Keep orange as an accent and let green and cream carry the page
  • Use real photos of real practices and people
  • Name the DPC and concierge model directly. We own that focus
  • Keep every claim specific and grounded

Don't

  • Use em dashes. Anywhere. Use periods, commas, or colons
  • Lean on filler like innovative, transform, elevate, leverage, seamless, or cutting-edge
  • Use rocket-ship or generic startup imagery
  • Use corporate blue-and-gray or sterile medical clipart
  • Sound like a generic digital agency
  • Flood a layout with orange
13 · Differentiation

Most agencies are generalists who took a healthcare client. We are specialists who happen to do marketing.

We only work in DPC and concierge medicine. That means you do not pay us to learn your model, we do not hand you a website built for a dentist, and we do not write copy that could belong to any clinic in the country.

We bring patterns that work across the practices we have launched, we understand the membership conversion problem, and we build for the local market you are competing in. That focus is the whole point.

14 · Sample Copy

Ready-to-use language

Homepage hero
You handle the medicine. We will handle the brand, the website, and the growth.

JumpStart builds branding, websites, and marketing for Direct Primary Care and concierge practices, so the patients you want can find you, understand you, and join.

Button: Book a strategy call

About section

JumpStart started with a simple frustration: great independent physicians were opening practices that almost no one could find or understand. The medicine was excellent. The marketing was an afterthought, a template, or nothing at all.

We built JumpStart to fix that one problem well. We work only with DPC and concierge practices, which means we already know the model, the questions patients ask, and the local-market reality you are up against. From naming and positioning to your website, your SEO, and the marketing that keeps running after launch, we give you a brand that does the explaining for you.

Services overview

Everything you need to launch and grow an independent practice, in one place. We start with strategy, who you serve and what makes you different, then build the brand, the website, and the marketing on top of it. Most clients work with us through launch and stay for ongoing growth.

Brand positioning statement

The branding and growth partner built specifically for DPC and concierge medicine.

Short company bio

JumpStart DPC Solutions is a healthcare branding and growth partner for independent primary care. We help physicians launch and grow Direct Primary Care and concierge practices with clear positioning, custom websites, SEO, and ongoing marketing. We work only in the DPC and concierge space, so the strategy is built for the model from day one. Simple. Tailored. Effective.

Long company bio

JumpStart DPC Solutions helps independent physicians build practices that patients understand, trust, and want to join. We specialize in Direct Primary Care and concierge medicine, and that focus shapes everything we do. We know the membership model can be hard to explain, that a new practice often needs members before it opens, and that most physicians did not go to medical school to become marketers.

Our work covers the full arc of a practice. We start with positioning: who you serve, what makes your care different, and how to say it in plain language. From there we build the brand, design and develop a custom website that explains your model and drives membership inquiries, set up local SEO, and run the ongoing marketing that keeps your practice visible after launch.

Founded by Eric Caballero, JumpStart works as a hands-on partner, not a vendor. We are practical, healthcare-specific, and direct. We help physicians who want to practice medicine on their own terms turn that vision into a real, sustainable business. Simple. Tailored. Effective.

Instagram bio

Branding, websites & growth built for DPC and concierge practices. Helping independent physicians launch and grow. Simple. Tailored. Effective.

LinkedIn company description

JumpStart DPC Solutions is a healthcare branding and growth partner for Direct Primary Care and concierge practices. We help independent physicians launch, brand, market, and grow practices that patients understand and want to join.

Our work spans brand positioning and strategy, visual identity, custom website development, local SEO and AEO, and ongoing marketing, from blogs and social to email and referral materials. We work only in the DPC and concierge space, so we bring the model knowledge most agencies do not.

If you are a physician building an independent practice, or an established practice that needs stronger branding and more consistent marketing, we would like to help. Simple. Tailored. Effective.

We work only with DPC and concierge practices, so what follows is shaped by that experience, not a generic template. Everything here is meant to be practical: clear scope, clear deliverables, and a plan you can actually run.

One-liner

SEO meta description (155 char)

JumpStart DPC Solutions builds branding, websites, and marketing for Direct Primary Care and concierge practices. Helping independent physicians grow.

Approx. 150 characters
SEO-friendly company description (long)

JumpStart DPC Solutions is a healthcare marketing and branding agency for Direct Primary Care and concierge practices. We help independent physicians with brand strategy, custom websites, local SEO, and ongoing marketing to launch and grow membership-based primary care practices.

Brand voice in 500 characters

JumpStart sounds like a trusted advisor who knows both medicine and marketing. We are clear, direct, and warm, never salesy or corporate. We explain things in plain language, skip the buzzwords, and respect the reader. We are confident without arrogance and strategic without jargon. We talk to physicians as peers and to patients as people. Every line should feel human, specific, and useful. If it reads like a generic agency or like an AI wrote it, it is wrong.

Visual style in 500 characters

JumpStart's look is clean, warm, and quietly premium. Deep sage green carries trust and calm. Warm orange adds energy as an accent, never the whole page. Cream keeps everything light and human. We pair a warm serif for headlines with a clean sans for body, with plenty of white space, simple line icons, and real photography over stock. Healthcare-adjacent but not sterile, professional but not corporate, modern but not techy. Growth and forward motion, handled with restraint.

Simple. Tailored. Effective.
